metadata.title: AI+1 — AI+1 series 1/4
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent; callout ink #161511 on cream. Do not retint.
metadata.greeting_note: English (HAI shortest-form budget) — HAI takes only the shortest forms (Hi · Ola · Hej · Ciao); never spell out HumanitariansAI in the greeting (the footer chip does that). Wagwan n/a (Bear only).
metadata.purpose: CUSTOMER pitch, not investor pitch: sell custom workshops, custom courses, and $1 Kindle books from a nonprofit doing good, and send viewers to the Humanitarians AI YouTube.
metadata.note: COLD OPEN LAW: B00 = ClaudeComposerAsk. SPARK-LINE LAW: every inner beat carries a short serif spark line. HANDOFF LAW: 2nd-to-last beat is the composer with a paste-ready action, greeting 'Your turn.'. OUTRO LAW: restate the title, poster serif, terracotta period. House grammar: >=1 PREDICT beat; verdict page carries the phase gate; decisions.json + DECISIONS.md ship beside this sheet.

beats.narration_text: AI plus one, in ninety seconds. Every course we make has a canonical version you can get today for about a dollar — and a custom version we build for you, that you own the rights to make yours. And it all comes from a nonprofit, so every purchase funds the mission. Here's the whole offer.

The canonical layer first. Every course is a textbook on Kindle for one dollar — free on Kindle Unlimited. Every lecture is on our YouTube, free. Every slide deck runs as a voiced, animated page on our site. That's not a sample. That's the whole course.

When you need your own version — your program, your examples, your name on it — you email info at humanitarians dot A I. We quote a price. You get a zip: the base videos, the exams, and the rights to transform it into your book, your course, your workshop.

The zip opens in any C L I — Claude Code, Codex, whichever you use. Point your tools at it and start editing: rename, reorder, rewrite, rebrand. You're not locked to our software, because there isn't any.
Quick check before the next part. If your world is a university learning system, guess what the zip exports to. Got a guess?

Canvas. The zip exports a dot I M S C C cartridge you upload directly — modules, videos, exams, in place. And if you'd rather we run it: custom training, or a white-label workshop your own experts deliver.

One page. The canon is a dollar. The custom version is a quote away, ships as a zip, opens in any C L I, lands in Canvas, and your experts can deliver it as a workshop. And every purchase funds a nonprofit's programs. That's AI plus one.

artifactLines: Canonical: $1 Kindle textbook (free on Kindle Unlimited) · free YouTube videos · voiced animated decks.
Custom: email info@humanitarians.ai for a quote — the zip ships base videos, exams, transform rights.
Yours: opens in any CLI · exports .imscc straight into Canvas · white-label workshops available.
From a 501(c)(3): purchases fund fellows, mentorship, and free AI education.
Phase gate: we build the base · you make it yours · your experts deliver it.

beats.narration_text: Your turn — this takes two minutes. Paste this and find your course. The dollar version is the honest demo; the custom version is one email after that.

props.command: List the Humanitarians AI courses at humanitarians.ai/courses, find the one closest to what I teach, and show me its three canonical forms: the $1 Kindle book, the YouTube playlist, and the voiced deck on the site.

beats.narration_text: AI plus one. The details are next — the canon, your version, and Canvas. And every course is watchable free on this channel.

props.title: AI+1.
props.subline: your course, your version · every course free on this channel
